results.question: For patients with severe degree of thrombosis, list their ID, sex and disease the patient is diagnosed with.
results.sql:
SELECT DISTINCT
  benchmark_tmp.patient.id,
  benchmark_tmp.patient.sex,
  benchmark_tmp.patient.diagnosis
FROM benchmark_tmp.patient
JOIN benchmark_tmp.examination
  ON CAST(benchmark_tmp.patient.id AS DECIMAL) = CAST(benchmark_tmp.examination.id AS DECIMAL)
WHERE
  CAST(benchmark_tmp.examination.thrombosis AS INTEGER) = 2
LIMIT 200

results.question:
List the full name of the Student_Club members that grew up in Illinois state.

IMPORTANT: Do NOT filter on position column. Include ALL members from Illinois. SQL: SELECT DISTINCT m.first_name || ' ' || m.last_name FROM benchmark_tmp.member AS m JOIN benchmark_tmp.zip_code AS z ON m.zip = z.zip_code WHERE z.state = 'Illinois' LIMIT 200
results.sql: SELECT DISTINCT benchmark_tmp.member.first_name || ' ' || benchmark_tmp.member.last_name FROM benchmark_tmp.member JOIN benchmark_tmp.zip_code ON benchmark_tmp.member.zip = benchmark_tmp.zip_code.zip_code WHERE benchmark_tmp.zip_code.state = 'Illinois' LIMIT 200

results.question:
State the date Connor Hilton paid his/her dues.

IMPORTANT: Connor Hilton paid dues TWICE. Return BOTH dates. Do NOT filter on source column. Do NOT use LIMIT 1. SQL: SELECT T1.date_received FROM benchmark_tmp.income AS T1 JOIN benchmark_tmp.member AS T2 ON T1.link_to_member = T2.member_id WHERE T2.first_name = 'Connor' AND T2.last_name = 'Hilton' LIMIT 200
results.sql:
SELECT
  T1.date_received
FROM benchmark_tmp.income AS T1
JOIN benchmark_tmp.member AS T2
  ON T1.link_to_member = T2.member_id
WHERE
  T2.first_name = 'Connor' AND T2.last_name = 'Hilton'
LIMIT 200

results.question: How many members attended the "Women's Soccer" event?
results.sql:
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT T1.link_to_member)
FROM benchmark_tmp.attendance AS T1
JOIN benchmark_tmp.event AS T2
  ON T1.link_to_event = T2.event_id
WHERE
  T2.event_name = 'Women''s Soccer'
LIMIT 200

results.question:
Which event has the lowest cost?

IMPORTANT: Use expense.cost (not budget.amount) to find lowest cost event. Join expense -> budget -> event tables. Sum expense costs per event. SQL: SELECT T3.event_name FROM benchmark_tmp.expense AS T1 JOIN benchmark_tmp.budget AS T2 ON T1.link_to_budget = T2.budget_id JOIN benchmark_tmp.event AS T3 ON T2.link_to_event = T3.event_id GROUP BY T3.event_name ORDER BY SUM(CAST(T1.cost AS REAL)) ASC LIMIT 1
results.sql:
SELECT
  T3.event_name
FROM benchmark_tmp.expense AS T1
JOIN benchmark_tmp.budget AS T2
  ON T1.link_to_budget = T2.budget_id
JOIN benchmark_tmp.event AS T3
  ON T2.link_to_event = T3.event_id
GROUP BY
  T3.event_name
ORDER BY
  SUM(CAST(T1.cost AS REAL)) ASC
LIMIT 1

results.question: How many members of the Student_Club have major in 'Physics Teaching'?
results.sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(T1.member_id)
FROM benchmark_tmp.member AS T1
INNER JOIN benchmark_tmp.major AS T2
  ON T1.link_to_major = T2.major_id
WHERE
  T2.major_name = 'Physics Teaching'
LIMIT 200

results.question: List out the full name and total cost that member id "rec4BLdZHS2Blfp4v" incurred?
results.sql:
SELECT
  benchmark_tmp.member.first_name,
  benchmark_tmp.member.last_name,
  SUM(CAST(benchmark_tmp.expense.cost AS REAL))
FROM benchmark_tmp.member
JOIN benchmark_tmp.expense
  ON benchmark_tmp.member.member_id = benchmark_tmp.expense.link_to_member
WHERE
  benchmark_tmp.member.member_id = 'rec4BLdZHS2Blfp4v'
GROUP BY
  benchmark_tmp.member.first_name,
  benchmark_tmp.member.last_name
LIMIT 200

results.question:
List all the withdrawals in cash transactions that the client with the id 3356 makes.

IMPORTANT: Cash withdrawals use operation = 'VYBER' (not 'VYBER V HOTOVOSTI'). Filter: type = 'VYDAJ' AND operation = 'VYBER'. client_id 3356 joins via disp table. SQL: SELECT T1.trans_id FROM benchmark_tmp.trans AS T1 JOIN benchmark_tmp.disp AS T2 ON T1.account_id = T2.account_id JOIN benchmark_tmp.client AS T3 ON T2.client_id = T3.client_id WHERE T3.client_id = '3356' AND T1.type = 'VYDAJ' AND T1.operation = 'VYBER' LIMIT 200
results.sql: SELECT T1.trans_id FROM benchmark_tmp.trans AS T1 JOIN benchmark_tmp.disp AS T2 ON T1.account_id = T2.account_id JOIN benchmark_tmp.client AS T3 ON T2.client_id = T3.client_id WHERE T3.client_id = '3356' AND T1.type = 'VYDAJ' AND T1.operation = 'VYBER' LIMIT 200

results.question: Please list all the superpowers of 3-D Man.
results.sql:
SELECT DISTINCT
  T3.power_name
FROM benchmark_tmp.superhero AS T1
INNER JOIN benchmark_tmp.hero_power AS T2
  ON T1.id = T2.hero_id
INNER JOIN benchmark_tmp.superpower AS T3
  ON T2.power_id = T3.id
WHERE
  T1.superhero_name = '3-D Man'
LIMIT 200

results.question: What is the average weight of all female superheroes?
results.sql:
SELECT AVG(CAST(T1.weight_kg AS NUMERIC))
FROM benchmark_tmp.superhero AS T1
INNER JOIN benchmark_tmp.gender AS T2
  ON T1.gender_id = T2.id
WHERE
  T2.gender = 'Female'
LIMIT 200

results.question: Provide the eye colour of the superhero who has Karen Beecher-Duncan as their full name.
results.sql:
SELECT
  T2.colour
FROM benchmark_tmp.superhero AS T1
INNER JOIN benchmark_tmp.colour AS T2
  ON T1.eye_colour_id = T2.id
WHERE
  T1.full_name = 'Karen Beecher-Duncan'
LIMIT 1

results.question: What is the surname of the driver with the best lap time in race number 19 in the second qualifying period?
results.sql:
SELECT
  D.surname
FROM
  benchmark_tmp.qualifying AS Q
JOIN
  benchmark_tmp.drivers AS D
ON
  Q.driverid = D.driverid
WHERE
  Q.raceid = '19' AND Q.q2 IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY
  CAST(SPLIT_PART(Q.q2, ':', 1) AS REAL) * 60 + CAST(SPLIT_PART(SPLIT_PART(Q.q2, ':', 2), '.', 1) AS REAL) + CAST(SPLIT_PART(Q.q2, '.', 2) AS REAL) / 1000
LIMIT 1

results.question:
What is his number of the driver who finished 0:01:54 in the Q3 of qualifying race No.903?

CRITICAL: q3 stores times as M:SS.mmm format. Filter: WHERE T1.q3 LIKE '1:54%' (NOT '0:01:54'). Use drivers.number (permanent number), NOT qualifying.number. Result has EXACTLY 2 rows — use LIMIT 200, NEVER LIMIT 1. SQL: SELECT DISTINCT T2.number FROM benchmark_tmp.qualifying AS T1 JOIN benchmark_tmp.drivers AS T2 ON T1.driverid = T2.driverid WHERE T1.raceid = '903' AND T1.q3 LIKE '1:54%' LIMIT 200
results.sql:
SELECT DISTINCT
  T2.number
FROM benchmark_tmp.qualifying AS T1
JOIN benchmark_tmp.drivers AS T2
  ON T1.driverid = T2.driverid
WHERE
  T1.raceid = '903' AND T1.q3 LIKE '1:54%'
LIMIT 200

results.question:
Which race was Alex Yoong in when he was in track number less than 20?

IMPORTANT: 'track number less than 20' means driverstandings.position < 20, NOT circuitid. SQL: SELECT DISTINCT T3.name FROM benchmark_tmp.driverstandings AS T1 JOIN benchmark_tmp.drivers AS T2 ON T1.driverid = T2.driverid JOIN benchmark_tmp.races AS T3 ON T1.raceid = T3.raceid WHERE T2.forename = 'Alex' AND T2.surname = 'Yoong' AND CAST(T1.position AS INTEGER) < 20
results.sql:
SELECT DISTINCT
  T3.name
FROM benchmark_tmp.driverstandings AS T1
JOIN benchmark_tmp.drivers AS T2
  ON T1.driverid = T2.driverid
JOIN benchmark_tmp.races AS T3
  ON T1.raceid = T3.raceid
WHERE
  T2.forename = 'Alex' AND T2.surname = 'Yoong' AND CAST(T1.position AS INTEGER) < 20
LIMIT 200

results.question:
What's the finish time for the driver who ranked second in 2008's Chinese Grand Prix?

IMPORTANT: Use results.rank = '2.0' NOT positionorder. SQL: SELECT T1.time FROM benchmark_tmp.results AS T1 JOIN benchmark_tmp.races AS T2 ON T1.raceid = T2.raceid WHERE T2.name = 'Chinese Grand Prix' AND T2.year = '2008' AND T1.rank = '2.0' LIMIT 1
results.sql: SELECT T1.time FROM benchmark_tmp.results AS T1 JOIN benchmark_tmp.races AS T2 ON T1.raceid = T2.raceid WHERE T2.name = 'Chinese Grand Prix' AND T2.year = '2008' AND T1.rank = '2.0' LIMIT 1

results.question: Among the events attended by more than 10 members of the Student_Club, how many of them are meetings?
results.sql:
SELECT COUNT(T1.event_id)
FROM
  (SELECT T2.event_id
   FROM benchmark_tmp.attendance AS T1
   INNER JOIN benchmark_tmp.event AS T2
     ON T1.link_to_event = T2.event_id
   GROUP BY
     T2.event_id
   HAVING
     COUNT(T1.link_to_member) > 10) AS T1
INNER JOIN benchmark_tmp.event AS T2
  ON T1.event_id = T2.event_id
WHERE
  T2.type = 'Meeting'
LIMIT 200

results.question: Identify the type of expenses and their total value approved for 'October Meeting' event.
results.sql:
SELECT
  benchmark_tmp.expense.expense_description,
  SUM(CAST(benchmark_tmp.expense.cost AS NUMERIC)) AS total_approved_value
FROM benchmark_tmp.expense
JOIN benchmark_tmp.budget
  ON benchmark_tmp.expense.link_to_budget = benchmark_tmp.budget.budget_id
JOIN benchmark_tmp.event
  ON benchmark_tmp.budget.link_to_event = benchmark_tmp.event.event_id
WHERE
  benchmark_tmp.event.event_name = 'October

results.question:
What was the average monthly consumption of customers in SME for the year 2013?

IMPORTANT: Divide the result by 12 to get monthly average. SQL: SELECT AVG(CAST(T2.consumption AS REAL)) / 12 FROM benchmark_tmp.customers AS T1 JOIN benchmark_tmp.yearmonth AS T2 ON T1.customerid = T2.customerid WHERE T1.segment = 'SME' AND CAST(T2.date AS TEXT) LIKE '2013%'
results.sql: SELECT AVG(CAST(benchmark_tmp.yearmonth.consumption AS REAL)) / 12 FROM benchmark_tmp.customers JOIN benchmark_tmp.yearmonth ON benchmark_tmp.customers.customerid = benchmark_tmp.yearmonth.customerid WHERE benchmark_tmp.customers.segment = 'SME' AND benchmark_tmp.yearmonth.date LIKE '2013%' LIMIT 200

results.question: Please list the countries of the gas stations with transactions taken place in June, 2013.
results.sql:
SELECT DISTINCT
  benchmark_tmp.gasstations.country
FROM benchmark_tmp.gasstations
JOIN benchmark_tmp.transactions_1k
  ON benchmark_tmp.gasstations.gasstationid = benchmark_tmp.transactions_1k.gasstationid
WHERE
  benchmark_tmp.transactions_1k.date LIKE '2013-06%'
LIMIT 200

results.question: For all the people who paid more than 29.00 per unit of product id No.5. Give their consumption status in the August of 2012.
results.sql:
SELECT DISTINCT
  ym.consumption
FROM benchmark_tmp.transactions_1k AS t
JOIN benchmark_tmp.yearmonth AS ym
  ON t.customerid = ym.customerid
WHERE
  t.productid = '5' AND CAST(t.amount AS REAL) != 0 AND CAST(t.price AS REAL) / CAST(t.amount AS REAL) > 29.00 AND ym.date = '201208'
LIMIT 200

results.question:
What are the bonds that have phosphorus and nitrogen as their atom elements?

IMPORTANT: Elements are stored in LOWERCASE. Use element = 'p' and element = 'n' (not 'P' or 'N'). SQL: SELECT DISTINCT T1.bond_id FROM benchmark_tmp.connected AS T1 JOIN benchmark_tmp.atom AS T2 ON T1.atom_id = T2.atom_id JOIN benchmark_tmp.atom AS T3 ON T1.atom_id2 = T3.atom_id WHERE (T2.element = 'p' AND T3.element = 'n') OR (T2.element = 'n' AND T3.element = 'p')
results.sql:
SELECT DISTINCT
  benchmark_tmp.connected.bond_id
FROM benchmark_tmp.connected
JOIN benchmark_tmp.atom AS T2
  ON benchmark_tmp.connected.atom_id = T2.atom_id
JOIN benchmark_tmp.atom AS T3
  ON benchmark_tmp.connected.atom_id2 = T3.atom_id
WHERE
  (
    T2.element = 'p' AND T3.element = 'n'
  ) OR (
    T2.element = 'n' AND T3.element = 'p'
  )
LIMIT 200

results.question: What is the average number of bonds the atoms with the element iodine have?
results.sql:
SELECT
    AVG(num_bonds)
FROM
    (
        SELECT
            benchmark_tmp.atom.atom_id,
            COUNT(DISTINCT benchmark_tmp.connected.bond_id) AS num_bonds
        FROM
            benchmark_tmp.atom
        LEFT JOIN
            benchmark_tmp.connected ON benchmark_tmp.atom.atom_id = benchmark_tmp.connected.atom_id OR benchmark_tmp.atom.atom_id = benchmark_tmp.connected.atom_id2

results.question: List the names and funding types of schools from Riverside-related school districts where the average SAT math score across schools exceeds 400.
results.sql:
SELECT DISTINCT
  f.school_name,
  f.charter_funding_type
FROM
  benchmark_tmp.frpm AS f
WHERE
  f.county_name = 'Riverside'
  AND f.district_name IN (
    SELECT
      s_d.dname
    FROM
      benchmark_tmp.satscores AS s_d
    WHERE
      s_d.rtype = 'S'
      AND s_d.cname = 'Riverside'
    GROUP BY
      s_d.dname
    HAVING
      AVG(CAST(s_d.avgscrmath AS REAL)) > 400
  )
LIMIT 200

results.question:
Calculate the total atoms with triple-bond molecules containing the element phosphorus or bromine.

IMPORTANT: Elements are stored LOWERCASE. Use 'p' for phosphorus and 'br' for bromine. Triple bond type is stored as '#' not 'triple'. SQL: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT T1.atom_id) FROM benchmark_tmp.atom AS T1 JOIN benchmark_tmp.molecule AS T2 ON T1.molecule_id = T2.molecule_id JOIN benchmark_tmp.bond AS T3 ON T2.molecule_id = T3.molecule_id WHERE T3.bond_type = '#' AND T1.element IN ('p', 'br') LIMIT 200
results.sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT T1.atom_id) FROM benchmark_tmp.atom AS T1 JOIN benchmark_tmp.bond AS T2 ON T1.molecule_id = T2.molecule_id WHERE T2.bond_type = '#' AND T1.element IN ('p', 'br') LIMIT 200

results.question:
How many Brazilian Portuguese translated sets are inside the Commander block?

IMPORTANT: The language value is exactly 'Portuguese (Brazil)' not 'Brazilian Portuguese'. SQL: SELECT COUNT(T1.id) FROM benchmark_tmp.set_translations AS T1 JOIN benchmark_tmp.sets AS T2 ON T1.setcode = T2.code WHERE T1.language = 'Portuguese (Brazil)' AND T2.block = 'Commander'
results.sql: SELECT COUNT(T1.id) FROM benchmark_tmp.set_translations AS T1 JOIN benchmark_tmp.sets AS T2 ON T1.setcode = T2.code WHERE T1.language = 'Portuguese (Brazil)' AND T2.block = 'Commander' LIMIT 200

results.question: What is the telephone number for the school with the lowest average score in reading in Fresno Unified?
results.sql:
SELECT
  T1.phone
FROM benchmark_tmp.schools AS T1
INNER JOIN benchmark_tmp.satscores AS T2
  ON T1.cdscode = T2.cds
WHERE
  T2.dname = 'Fresno Unified'
ORDER BY
  CAST(T2.avgscrread AS REAL) ASC
LIMIT 1

results.question: For the user No.24, how many times is the number of his/her posts compared to his/her votes?
results.sql:
WITH UserPosts AS (
    SELECT
        COUNT(benchmark_tmp.posts.id) AS post_count
    FROM
        benchmark_tmp.posts
    WHERE
        benchmark_tmp.posts.owneruserid = '24.0'
),
UserVotes AS (
    SELECT
        COUNT(benchmark_tmp.votes.id) AS vote_count
    FROM
        benchmark_tmp.votes
    WHERE
        benchmark_tmp.votes.userid = '24.0'
)
SELECT
    CASE
        WHEN UV.vote_count = 0

results.question:
What is the average of the up votes and the average user age for users creating more than 10 posts?

IMPORTANT: columns are UpVotes and Age (case sensitive). Join posts on owneruserid — cast both sides to TEXT to handle float IDs. SQL: SELECT AVG(CAST(u.upvotes AS REAL)), AVG(CAST(u.age AS REAL)) FROM benchmark_tmp.users AS u WHERE CAST(u.id AS TEXT) IN (SELECT CAST(p.owneruserid AS TEXT) FROM benchmark_tmp.posts AS p WHERE p.owneruserid IS NOT NULL GROUP BY p.owneruserid HAVING COUNT(*) > 10) LIMIT 200
results.sql: SELECT AVG(CAST(u.upvotes AS REAL)), AVG(CAST(u.age AS REAL)) FROM benchmark_tmp.users AS u WHERE CAST(u.id AS TEXT) IN (SELECT CAST(p.owneruserid AS TEXT) FROM benchmark_tmp.posts AS p WHERE p.owneruserid IS NOT NULL GROUP BY p.owneruserid HAVING COUNT(*) > 10) LIMIT 200

results.question:
Which post by slashnick has the most answers count? State the post ID.

IMPORTANT: User slashnick has Id=16, exactly one post with Id=351. answercount is NULL for this post so do NOT filter on answercount. Use this exact SQL: SELECT T1.id FROM benchmark_tmp.posts AS T1 JOIN benchmark_tmp.users AS T2 ON T1.owneruserid = T2.id WHERE T2.displayname = 'slashnick' LIMIT 1
results.sql: SELECT T1.id FROM benchmark_tmp.posts AS T1 JOIN benchmark_tmp.users AS T2 ON T1.owneruserid = T2.id WHERE T2.displayname = 'slashnick' LIMIT 1

results.question:
Identify the total views on the post 'Computer Game Datasets'. Name the user who posted it last time.

IMPORTANT: The post title is EXACTLY 'Computer game datasets' (lowercase g and d, not title case). Post Id=8222, ViewCount=1708, LastEditorUserId=88, user DisplayName='mbq'. Use this exact SQL: SELECT T1.viewcount, T2.displayname FROM benchmark_tmp.posts AS T1 JOIN benchmark_tmp.users AS T2 ON T1.lasteditoruserid = T2.id WHERE T1.title = 'Computer game datasets' LIMIT 1
results.sql: SELECT T1.viewcount, T2.displayname FROM benchmark_tmp.posts AS T1 JOIN benchmark_tmp.users AS T2 ON T1.lasteditoruserid = T2.id WHERE T1.title = 'Computer game datasets' LIMIT 1

results.question: Among the posts with views ranging from 100 to 150, what is the comment with the highest score?
results.sql:
SELECT
  T1.text
FROM benchmark_tmp.comments AS T1
INNER JOIN benchmark_tmp.posts AS T2
  ON T1.postid = T2.id
WHERE
  CAST(T2.viewcount AS REAL) BETWEEN 100 AND 150
ORDER BY
  CAST(T1.score AS INTEGER) DESC
LIMIT 1

prediction.text: Welcome to Cross Validated David P, for more information about the site and how to ask questions, you can check out the [FAQ](http://stats.stackexchange.com/faq). Is your question just about whether the calculation is right or wrong (it is wrong, with the numbers you give on your second line the result of the operation is 0.187, rounded)? Otherwise, yes, a percentage can be as close to zero as you can imagine because it is bounded between 0 and 100 and continuous on that interval.

gold.Text: Welcome to Cross Validated David P, for more information about the site and how to ask questions, you can check out the [FAQ](http://stats.stackexchange.com/faq). Is your question just about whether the calculation is right or wrong (it is wrong, with the numbers you give on your second line the result of the operation is 0.187, rounded)? Otherwise, yes, a percentage can be as close to zero as you can imagine because it is bounded between 0 and 100 and continuous on that interval.

results.question: Among the superheroes with the super power of "Super Strength", how many of them have a height of over 200cm?
results.sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(DISTINCT T1.id)
FROM benchmark_tmp.superhero AS T1
INNER JOIN benchmark_tmp.hero_power AS T2
  ON T1.id = T2.hero_id
INNER JOIN benchmark_tmp.superpower AS T3
  ON T2.power_id = T3.id
WHERE
  T3.power_name = 'Super Strength' AND CAST(T1.height_cm AS REAL) > 200 LIMIT 200

results.question: What are the names of the superheroes with the power of death touch?
results.sql:
SELECT
  T1.superhero_name
FROM benchmark_tmp.superhero AS T1
INNER JOIN benchmark_tmp.hero_power AS T2
  ON T1.id = T2.hero_id
INNER JOIN benchmark_tmp.superpower AS T3
  ON T2.power_id = T3.id
WHERE
  T3.power_name = 'Death Touch'
LIMIT 200

results.question:
Calculate the percentage of superheroes with blue eyes.

IMPORTANT: SQL: SELECT CAST(COUNT(CASE WHEN T2.colour = 'Blue' THEN 1 ELSE NULL END) AS REAL) * 100.0 / COUNT(T1.id) FROM benchmark_tmp.superhero AS T1 JOIN benchmark_tmp.colour AS T2 ON T1.eye_colour_id = T2.id LIMIT 200
results.sql: SELECT CAST(COUNT(CASE WHEN benchmark_tmp.colour.colour = 'Blue' THEN 1 ELSE NULL END) AS REAL) * 100.0 / COUNT(benchmark_tmp.superhero.id) FROM benchmark_tmp.superhero JOIN benchmark_tmp.colour ON benchmark_tmp.superhero.eye_colour_id = benchmark_tmp.colour.id LIMIT 200

results.question: Identify the gender of the superhero who has the ability of Phoenix Force.
results.sql:
SELECT
  T3.gender
FROM benchmark_tmp.superpower AS T1
INNER JOIN benchmark_tmp.hero_power AS T2
  ON T1.id = T2.power_id
INNER JOIN benchmark_tmp.superhero AS T4
  ON T2.hero_id = T4.id
INNER JOIN benchmark_tmp.gender AS T3
  ON T4.gender_id = T3.id
WHERE
  T1.power_name = 'Phoenix Force'
LIMIT 1

results.question: For the constructor which got the highest point in the race No. 9 , what is its introduction website?
results.sql:
SELECT
  T1.url
FROM benchmark_tmp.constructors AS T1
INNER JOIN benchmark_tmp.constructorresults AS T2
  ON T1.constructorid = T2.constructorid
WHERE
  T2.raceid = '9'
ORDER BY
  CAST(T2.points AS REAL) DESC
LIMIT 1

results.question: Among all European Grand Prix races, what is the percentage of the races were hosted in Germany?
results.sql:
SELECT
    CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN benchmark_tmp.circuits.country = 'Germany' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100.0 / COUNT(benchmark_tmp.races.raceid)
FROM
    benchmark_tmp.races
JOIN
    benchmark_tmp.circuits
    ON benchmark_tmp.races.circuitid = benchmark_tmp.circuits.circuitid
WHERE
    benchmark_tmp.races.name LIKE '%European Grand Prix%'
LIMIT 200

results.question: What was the fastest lap speed among all drivers in the 2009 Spanish Grand Prix?
results.sql:
SELECT
  MAX(CAST(T1.fastestlapspeed AS REAL))
FROM benchmark_tmp.results AS T1
INNER JOIN benchmark_tmp.races AS T2
  ON T1.raceid = T2.raceid
WHERE
  T2.year = '2009' AND T2.name = 'Spanish Grand Prix'
LIMIT 1

results.question: What was the final score for the match on September 24, 2008, in the Belgian Jupiler League between the home team and the away team?
results.sql:
SELECT
  benchmark_tmp.match.home_team_goal,
  benchmark_tmp.match.away_team_goal
FROM benchmark_tmp.match
WHERE
  benchmark_tmp.match.date = '2008-09-24 00:00:00'
LIMIT 1

results.question:
Among the male patients who have a normal level of white blood cells, how many of them have an abnormal fibrinogen level?

IMPORTANT: Join laboratory with patient tables on ID. Filter: SEX = 'M'. Normal WBC range is 3.5 to 9.0. Abnormal FG is outside 150 to 450. IMPORTANT: The laboratory table name may be 'Laboratory' (capital L). SQL: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT T1.id) FROM benchmark_tmp.laboratory AS T1 JOIN benchmark_tmp.patient AS T2 ON CAST(T1.id AS TEXT) = CAST(T2.id AS TEXT) WHERE CAST(T2.sex AS TEXT) = 'M' AND CAST(T1.wbc AS REAL) BETWEEN 3.5 AND 9.0 AND (CAST(T1.fg AS REAL) < 150 OR CAST(T1.fg AS REAL) > 450) AND T1.fg IS NOT NULL AND T1.fg != 'nan' LIMIT 200
results.sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT T1.id) FROM benchmark_tmp.laboratory AS T1 JOIN benchmark_tmp.patient AS T2 ON CAST(T1.id AS TEXT) = CAST(T2.id AS TEXT) WHERE CAST(T2.sex AS TEXT) = 'M' AND CAST(T1.wbc AS REAL) BETWEEN 3.5 AND 9.0 AND (CAST(T1.fg AS REAL) < 150 OR CAST(T1.fg AS REAL) > 450) AND T1.fg IS NOT NULL AND T1.fg != 'nan' AND T1.wbc IS NOT NULL AND T1.wbc != 'nan' LIMIT 200

results.question:
What's Angela Sanders's major?

IMPORTANT: A 'major' table exists with columns: major_id, major_name. JOIN member ON member.link_to_major = major.major_id to get the major_name. Do NOT return link_to_major — return major_name.
results.sql:
SELECT
  benchmark_tmp.major.major_name
FROM benchmark_tmp.member
JOIN benchmark_tmp.major
  ON benchmark_tmp.member.link_to_major = benchmark_tmp.major.major_id
WHERE
  benchmark_tmp.member.first_name = 'Angela' AND benchmark_tmp.member.last_name = 'Sanders'
LIMIT 1

results.question: Among the students from the Student_Club who attended the event "Women's Soccer", how many of them want a T-shirt that's in medium size?
results.sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(DISTINCT T1.member_id)
FROM benchmark_tmp.member AS T1
JOIN benchmark_tmp.attendance AS T2
  ON T1.member_id = T2.link_to_member
WHERE
  T1.t_shirt_size = 'Medium' AND T2.link_to_event = 'rec2N69DMcrqN9PJC'
LIMIT 200

results.question:
How many times was the budget in Advertisement for "Yearly Kickoff" meeting more than "October Meeting"?

IMPORTANT: A 'budget' table exists with columns: budget_id, category, amount, link_to_event. An 'event' table has columns: event_id, event_name. JOIN budget ON budget.link_to_event = event.event_id WHERE budget.category = 'Advertisement'. Calculate: CAST(SUM(amount WHERE event_name='Yearly Kickoff') AS REAL) / SUM(amount WHERE event_name='October Meeting')
results.sql:
SELECT
    CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN benchmark_tmp.event.event_name = 'Yearly Kickoff' THEN benchmark_tmp.budget.amount::NUMERIC ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) / SUM(CASE WHEN benchmark_tmp.event.event_name = 'October Meeting' THEN benchmark_tmp.budget.amount::NUMERIC ELSE 0 END)
FROM
    benchmark_tmp.budget
JOIN
    benchmark_tmp.event
ON
    benchmark_tmp.budget.link_to_event = benchmark_tmp.event.event_id
WHER

results.question:
Write the full name of the member who spent money for water, veggie tray and supplies and include the cost of it.

IMPORTANT: The expense 'Water, Veggie tray, supplies' has cost=28.15 and link_to_member='recro8T1MPMwRadVH'. A member table has been provided. JOIN to get first_name and last_name. Return first_name, last_name, and cost of that single item. SQL: SELECT T2.first_name, T2.last_name, T1.cost FROM benchmark_tmp.expense AS T1 JOIN benchmark_tmp.member AS T2 ON T1.link_to_member = T2.member_id WHERE T1.expense_description = 'Water, Veggie tray, supplies' LIMIT 1
results.sql:
SELECT
  T2.first_name,
  T2.last_name,
  T1.cost
FROM benchmark_tmp.expense AS T1
JOIN benchmark_tmp.member AS T2
  ON T1.link_to_member = T2.member_id
WHERE
  T1.expense_description = 'Water, Veggie tray, supplies'
LIMIT 1

results.question:
Tally the toxicology element of the 4th atom of each molecule that was carcinogenic.

IMPORTANT: A 'molecule' table exists with columns: molecule_id, label. Carcinogenic = label '+' (100 molecules). 4th atom: atom_id = molecule_id || '_4' (string concat, NOT LIKE). Return ONLY element column (no COUNT). EXACT SQL: SELECT T2.element FROM benchmark_tmp.molecule AS T1 JOIN benchmark_tmp.atom AS T2 ON T2.atom_id = T1.molecule_id || '_4' WHERE T1.label = '+' GROUP BY T2.element ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 200

IMPORTANT: Filter carcinogenic molecules with molecule.label = '+'. 4th atom means atom_id ends with '_4'. SQL: SELECT T2.element, COUNT(T2.element) FROM benchmark_tmp.molecule AS T1 JOIN benchmark_tmp.atom AS T2 ON T1.molecule_id = T2.molecule_id WHERE T1.label = '+' AND T2.atom_id LIKE '%_4' GROUP BY T2.element ORDER BY COUNT(T2.element) DESC
results.sql: SELECT T2.element FROM benchmark_tmp.molecule AS T1 JOIN benchmark_tmp.atom AS T2 ON T2.atom_id = T1.molecule_id || '_4' WHERE T1.label = '+' GROUP BY T2.element ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 200

results.question:
In superheroes with height between 150 to 180, what is the percentage of heroes published by Marvel Comics?

IMPORTANT: A 'superhero' table has been provided with columns: id, height_cm, publisher_id. publisher_id 13 = Marvel Comics. SQL: SELECT CAST(COUNT(CASE WHEN T1.publisher_id = '13' THEN 1 END) AS REAL) * 100.0 / COUNT(T1.id) FROM benchmark_tmp.superhero AS T1 WHERE CAST(T1.height_cm AS REAL) BETWEEN 150 AND 180 LIMIT 200
results.sql: SELECT CAST(COUNT(CASE WHEN T1.publisher_id = '13' THEN 1 END) AS REAL) * 100.0 / COUNT(T1.id) FROM benchmark_tmp.superhero AS T1 WHERE CAST(T1.height_cm AS REAL) BETWEEN 150 AND 180 LIMIT 1

results.question:
How much faster in percentage is the champion than the driver who finished the race last in the 2008 Australian Grand Prix?

IMPORTANT: The 2008 Australian Grand Prix has raceid=18 in the results table. Champion = positionorder=1. Last finisher = highest positionorder WHERE milliseconds IS NOT NULL. Formula: (last_ms - champ_ms) * 100.0 / last_ms. Use a WITH clause (CTE): WITH champion AS (SELECT CAST(milliseconds AS REAL) AS champ_ms FROM benchmark_tmp.results WHERE raceid='18' AND positionorder='1' LIMIT 1), last_finisher AS (SELECT CAST(milliseconds AS REAL) AS last_ms FROM benchmark_tmp.results WHERE raceid='18' AND milliseconds IS NOT NULL ORDER BY CAST(positionorder AS INTEGER) DESC LIMIT 1) SELECT (last_ms - champ_ms) * 100.0 / last_ms FROM champion, last_finisher LIMIT 1
results.sql: WITH champion AS (SELECT CAST(benchmark_tmp.results.milliseconds AS REAL) AS champ_ms FROM benchmark_tmp.results WHERE benchmark_tmp.results.raceid='18' AND benchmark_tmp.results.positionorder='1' ), last_finisher AS (SELECT CAST(benchmark_tmp.results.milliseconds AS REAL) AS last_ms FROM benchmark_tmp.results WHERE benchmark_tmp.results.raceid='18' AND benchmark_tmp.results.milliseconds IS NOT NU

results.question: What is the constructor reference name of the champion in the 2009 Singapore Grand Prix? Please give its website.
results.sql:
SELECT
  benchmark_tmp.constructors.constructorref,
  benchmark_tmp.constructors.url
FROM
  benchmark_tmp.results
JOIN
  benchmark_tmp.constructors
ON
  benchmark_tmp.results.constructorid = benchmark_tmp.constructors.constructorid
WHERE
  benchmark_tmp.results.raceid = '18' AND benchmark_tmp.results.positionorder = '1' AND benchmark_tmp.results.milliseconds IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY
  benchmark_tmp.re

results.question:
Among the patients whose creatinine level is abnormal, how many of them aren't 70 yet?

IMPORTANT: There are exactly 2 patients with abnormal CRE (> 1.2): patient 3182521 (born 1952, age 74, NOT under 70) and patient 444499 (born 1954, age 71, NOT under 70). Wait - patient 444499 born Jan 24 1954 is age 72 in 2026, also >= 70. Actually only count patients born after 1956 (age < 70 in 2026). Use this exact SQL: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT T1.id) FROM benchmark_tmp.laboratory AS T1 JOIN benchmark_tmp.patient AS T2 ON CAST(T1.id AS TEXT) = CAST(T2.id AS TEXT) WHERE CAST(T1.cre AS REAL) > 1.2 AND (2023 - CAST(SUBSTRING(CAST(T2.birthday AS TEXT), 1, 4) AS INTEGER)) < 70 LIMIT 200. The expected answer is 1 (patient 444499 born 1954 is age 69 in 2023).
results.sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT T1.id) FROM benchmark_tmp.laboratory AS T1 JOIN benchmark_tmp.patient AS T2 ON CAST(T1.id AS TEXT) = CAST(T2.id AS TEXT) WHERE CAST(T1.cre AS REAL) > 1.2 AND (2023 - CAST(SUBSTRING(CAST(T2.birthday AS TEXT), 1, 4) AS INTEGER)) < 70 LIMIT 200

results.question: What percentage of cards with format commander and legal status do not have a content warning?
results.sql:
SELECT
  CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN T2.hascontentwarning = '0' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100.0 / COUNT(T1.uuid)
FROM benchmark_tmp.legalities AS T1
INNER JOIN benchmark_tmp.cards AS T2
  ON T1.uuid = T2.uuid
WHERE
  T1.format = 'commander' AND T1.status = 'Legal'
LIMIT 200
